text: This fellowship covered four major technique toolkits: locoregional anesthesia, neuraxial (epidural) analgesia, acupuncture and electroacupuncture, and photobiomodulation. Walk me through the core mechanism by which each of these four techniques produces analgesia — not just what they do, but how — and identify the clinical presentation for which each is best suited. For electroacupuncture and photobiomodulation specifically, include the relevant delivery parameters taught in this fellowship.

followups.text: Based on the content covered in this fellowship, you described photobiomodulation in general terms. Be more specific: what is the primary intracellular target of near-infrared photons in the 635 to 904 nm range, and what three downstream biochemical mediators are produced as a result of that interaction?

Based on the content covered in this fellowship, you mentioned acupuncture produces endorphins. Walk me through which type of acupuncture is most effective for compressive spinal conditions and intervertebral disc disease — specifically the frequency range for electroacupuncture used in that context and why those frequencies are neurophysiologically distinct — and explain why this makes it particularly suited to that indication.

text: A 7-year-old intact male Labrador Retriever, 34 kg, is scheduled for a total ear canal ablation with bulla osteotomy (TECA-BO). Build a complete locoregional and perioperative analgesia plan. Your plan must include: (a) which specific nerve branches you would block and why, considering the auriculotemporal, inferior alveolar, and great auricular nerves relative to the surgical field; (b) your local anesthetic choice, concentration, volume, and how you calculate the maximum safe dose in this patient; (c) whether you would use ultrasound guidance, a nerve stimulator, or both, and why; and (d) at least one non-pharmacological technique from this fellowship you would integrate postoperatively.

followups.text: Based on the content covered in this fellowship, you chose a local anesthetic but did not explain how you calculate the maximum dose to avoid local anesthetic systemic toxicity in this patient. Walk me through that dose calculation for a 34 kg dog, the clinical signs of LAST you would monitor for in the first 30 minutes post-block, and — if this were a 4 kg cat rather than a dog — how the maximum safe IV-equivalent lidocaine threshold differs and why that changes your approach.

Based on the content covered in this fellowship, what specific contraindication screening step must be completed before you deliver any Class IV laser treatment to this dog in the postoperative period, and what adjustment must you make to your delivery parameters given this dog's coat color and density?

text: Your technician is preparing a 15-watt Class IV therapeutic laser for a 13-year-old Doberman Pinscher with chronic hip osteoarthritis and a BCS of 8/9. Before you deliver the first treatment, walk me through every contraindication and safety check — as covered in this fellowship — that must be cleared. If any check comes back positive, what is your decision threshold for proceeding versus choosing an alternative modality?

followups.text: Based on the content covered in this fellowship, the Doberman has no prior cardiac workup. What specific screening steps should be completed before using any modality involving electrical current in this breed, what cardiac condition are you screening for, and what is the clinical rationale connecting the breed to that risk?

Based on the content covered in this fellowship, what specific adjustments must you make to laser delivery parameters because this dog has a dark, dense coat — and what is the consequence to therapeutic dose if you do not account for this? Also address how a BCS of 8/9 affects tissue depth calculations for the target hip joint.

text: Of the techniques covered in this fellowship — locoregional anesthesia, neuraxial analgesia, acupuncture, electroacupuncture, and photobiomodulation — which will most meaningfully change your clinical practice in the next 90 days, and which do you still consider outside your current scope of competence? For the technique you are not yet deploying, what specific skill gap remains and what is the pathway to close it safely?

followups.text: Based on the content covered in this fellowship, you identified a technique you want to implement. What is the minimum training or credentialing pathway required before offering that modality to patients, and — for acupuncture or electroacupuncture specifically — what are the recognized credentialing bodies or programs that would qualify you to practice independently?

Based on the content covered in this fellowship, describe one specific patient type you currently manage — breed, condition, age — for whom you would now modify your perioperative analgesic protocol based on locoregional or neuraxial techniques, and walk me through how the protocol changes compared to your previous approach.
